summary: Good Tobin matrix capture of a mixed‑bag ’85 show – standout Me & My Uncle, Big River and a soaring Comes A Time, but the second half drifts at times.
review: The Tobin matrix recording is clean and captures the audience energy better than any existing soundboard, making the set enjoyable despite some sonic quirks. The performance swings between high points – a hot first‑set run featuring Me & My Uncle, Big River and He's Gone, and an inspired second‑set with Comes A Time, Lost Sailor and an electrifying Gloria – and weaker moments where songs feel clunky or off‑key. Overall the show is uneven but rewarding for fans of the mid‑80s Dead.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is mixed (one reviewer 3★, another 5★) while recording quality is consistently praised; weighted average yields a solid mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Tobin matrix combines audience ambience with soundboard feed; reviewers call it "fantastic" and superior to the official release, though older SBD sources suffer ringing artifacts that do not affect this matrix.

poor_songs: Gimme Some Lovin' (anti‑climactic in one review)
She Belongs To Me (rough in one review)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set starts upbeat and builds a strong groove; second set opens strongly with Comes A Time but later becomes uneven, alternating between inspired jams and lackluster covers.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is praised on Stagger Lee, Big River, Jack Straw and the epic Comes A Time, though a few wrong notes and lyric slips are noted later (Day Job).
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh provides solid bass throughout; his shouted “Lovin!” at the end of Gimme Some Lovin' gets special mention.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s vocals shine on She Belongs To Me and he leads Lost Sailor with a compelling vibe, though one reviewer felt he was left out early in the set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s keyboards are highlighted for adding sparkle to Big River, driving Dough Knees, phrasing on He's Gone and contributing heavily to the second‑set improvisations.
